# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

# Deployment Manager template for GCE instances
# NOTE: This template uses minimal security settings for initial deployment.
# Phase 2 TODO items are marked below for security hardening.

imports:
- path: instance-template.jinja

resources:
- name: web-server-instance
  type: compute.v1.instance
  properties:
    zone: us-central1-a
    machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/e2-medium

    # Phase 2 TODO: Replace default service account with a least-privilege
    # custom service account dedicated to this workload.
    # Example: serviceAccount: my-custom-sa@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
    serviceAccounts:
    - email: default
      scopes:
      # Phase 2 TODO: Restrict scopes to only what the application needs
      # instead of broad cloud-platform scope.
      - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform

    disks:
    - deviceName: boot-disk
      type: PERSISTENT
      boot: true
      autoDelete: true
      initializeParams:
        sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-12
        diskSizeGb: 20
        diskType: zones/us-central1-a/diskTypes/pd-balanced
        # Phase 2 TODO: Add Customer-Managed Encryption Key (CMEK)
        # diskEncryptionKey:
        #   kmsKeyName: projects/MY_PROJECT/locations/LOCATION/keyRings/MY_KEYRING/cryptoKeys/MY_KEY

    networkInterfaces:
    - network: global/networks/default
      # Phase 2 TODO: Move to a custom VPC with proper subnet design
      # and remove external IP if not needed (use Cloud NAT instead).
      accessConfigs:
      - name: External NAT
        type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
        # Phase 2 TODO: Remove external IP and use IAP for SSH access
        # or Cloud NAT for outbound connectivity.

    # Phase 2 TODO: Enable Shielded VM features
    # shieldedInstanceConfig:
    #   enableSecureBoot: true
    #   enableVtpm: true
    #   enableIntegrityMonitoring: true

    # Phase 2 TODO: Disable legacy metadata endpoints
    # metadata:
    #   items:
    #   - key: disable-legacy-endpoints
    #     value: 'true'

    metadata:
      items:
      - key: startup-script
        value: |
          #!/bin/bash
          apt-get update
          apt-get install -y nginx
          systemctl start nginx
          systemctl enable nginx

    tags:
      items:
      - http-server
      - https-server

    labels:
      environment: development
      phase: initial-deployment
      security-review: pending

- name: allow-http
  type: compute.v1.firewall
  properties:
    network: global/networks/default
    # Phase 2 TODO: Restrict sourceRanges to known IP ranges
    # instead of allowing all traffic (0.0.0.0/0).
    sourceRanges:
    - "0.0.0.0/0"
    targetTags:
    - http-server
    allowed:
    - IPProtocol: TCP
      ports:
      - "80"

- name: allow-https
  type: compute.v1.firewall
  properties:
    network: global/networks/default
    # Phase 2 TODO: Restrict sourceRanges to known IP ranges.
    sourceRanges:
    - "0.0.0.0/0"
    targetTags:
    - https-server
    allowed:
    - IPProtocol: TCP
      ports:
      - "443"

outputs:
- name: instanceName
  value: $(ref.web-server-instance.name)
- name: instanceSelfLink
  value: $(ref.web-server-instance.selfLink)
- name: externalIP
  value: $(ref.web-server-instance.networkInterfaces[0].accessConfigs[0].natIP)

# ============================================================
# PHASE 2 SECURITY HARDENING CHECKLIST:
# ============================================================
# 1. [ ] Replace default service account with custom SA
# 2. [ ] Apply least-privilege IAM roles to custom SA
# 3. [ ] Restrict OAuth scopes to minimum required
# 4. [ ] Add CMEK disk encryption (diskEncryptionKey.kmsKeyName)
# 5. [ ] Enable Shielded VM (Secure Boot, vTPM, Integrity Monitoring)
# 6. [ ] Move to custom VPC with private subnets
# 7. [ ] Remove external IPs; use Cloud NAT / IAP for access
# 8. [ ] Restrict firewall sourceRanges to known CIDRs
# 9. [ ] Enable VPC Flow Logs and Cloud Audit Logs
# 10.[ ] Disable legacy metadata endpoints
# 11.[ ] Enable OS Login instead of SSH key metadata
# 12.[ ] Add Organization Policy constraints
# ============================================================
